entry.title: Re: EWM-Ware House Process Type
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to back-date Achievements in Continuou...
entry.content: Thank you so much Tina (and Nina!), this is absolutely the solve to my question! I've made the update and was able to confirm it works, thank you so much again!

entry.title: Re: SAPI to ODP extractor conversion
entry.content: Hi Savio, thanks for your reply. Copy TRFN to new ODP ds is fine... more important questions is not losing delta when switching from SAPI to ODP

entry.title: Deploy Typescript-SAPUI5 Application to BTP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to deploy a SAPUI5 (Fiori) Application to the Business Technology Platform. The Application was built with Typescript, will be deployed via the managed approuter and does not contain a CAP-Service.

For a SAPUI5 Application that uses Javascript, I used a combination of the following commands:

from the package.json

mbt build --mtar <projectname>.mtar  && cf deploy mta_archives/<projectname>.mtar --abort-on-error --retries 0

from the mta.yaml

[...]

commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf

[...]

This resulted in a build version of the app beeing created in the /dist-folder, containing a zipped file  <applicationname>.zip, that is created via the custom Task: ui5-task-zipper defined in the ui5.yaml. As part of the deployment process, this file is copied to the /resources-folder and from there deployed to the BTP.

Now with Typescript:

I am using the same command in the package.json. But with the following ones in the mta.yaml:

[...]

commands:
    - npm install
    - ui5 build --all --clean-dest

[...]

This results in a correctly built typescript application in the /dist-folder, but is missing the .zip-version, because the ui5-task-zipper is not executed from the ui5.yaml. (So obviously no file can be copied to the resources-folder)

If I now add the configuration from the ui5.yaml by adapting the script:

[...]

commands:
    - npm install
    - ui5 build --all --clean-dest --config ui5.yaml

[...]

The ui5-task-zipper creates a "normal" SAPUI5-application in the /dist-folder, missing the conversion from typescript to javascript, but with a .zip-file. If this version is deployed, the application will can not be started, because it contains a Component.ts instead of a Component.js.

Can someone tell me how the correct deployment process looks like?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Lukas

entry.title: EDocument Cockpit "Mapping Requested" Application Error
entry.content:
Hello Experts, I'm currently trying to implement the FI-Invoice in the eDocument Cockpit. 

Current State is the XML Generation/Preview with AIF. 

Im getting the following error :

Any idea how to fix this error? 

 

Br,
Pablo

entry.title: How to Follow Candidates in New Applicant WorkBench?
entry.content: We are not seeing the following the option to use Action Menu in Candidate List or to use Take action on Candidate Page in the new Applicant Workbench.  See attachment from Legacy. How does this work with the New Applicant Workbench - we are not seeing in guides, nor we can't find when testing.  Has anyone else experienced this?

entry.title: Re: EWM-Ware House Process Type
entry.content: Hi, Do you mean that the new Ware House Process should I uncheck the indicator "Select HU Without Storage Process",

entry.title: Where can I learn the configuration and administration of SAP Fieldglass Rates 2.0?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with SAP Fieldglass Rates 2.0 learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the configuration and administration of SAP Fieldglass Rates 2.0.

Learning objectives
Upon completing this course, administrators will be able to:

  • Examine the structure of a rate in the Rates 2.0 configuration
  • Learn the role of lookups when using Rates 2.0
  • Discover how Rates 2.0 provides greater flexibility and efficiency over Rates 1.0
  • Configure the Rates 2.0 structure, including creating a custom lookup
  • See how a Rates 2.0 configuration can co-exist with an existing Rates 1.0 configuration

Prerequisites

  • Introducing SAP Fieldglass

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge
We have also related SAP Fieldglass live sessions or this topic - register now 


Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: EWM-Ware House Process Type
entry.content: Hi, Do you mean that the new Ware House Process should I uncheck the indicator "Select HU Without Storage Process",

entry.title: SAP Build Workzone Standard Edition - Content Package Role
entry.content:
Hi,

Referring to this guide "A Guide to Developing and Deploying Content Packag... - SAP Community" by @jens_kristen : 

 I have issues with the roles in the Content Package and couldn't manage to get that solved.

When creating the Content Package, an error appears "Error: No src config for sample-role", but it continues with building the package. Also I get an error in Workzone. Could you please advise what could be the issue here? Many thanks! Max 

My role.json looks like this:

Content.json

entry.title: SIEM operations in action - Organization of SAP ETD operations and benefits on the security level
entry.content: In addition to regulatory requirements and the implementation of international security standards, the increasing number of cyber-attacks requires organizations to effectively operate Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) tools in their Security Operations Centers (SOCs) to respond to critical events in their IT landscape as quickly and efficiently as possible. In this session, we will discuss how to run SAP Enterprise Threat Detection (ETD) on a day-to-day basis in collaboration with a managed security service provider, how to implement regulatory requirements, and how to set up an effective response to critical system activities. We will also discuss the step-by-step integration of new system components (e.g. Cloud Connectors, SAP Success Factors) into ETD and how to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of the SOC team through employee training (table top exercises). Finally, we will show you how the daily operation of our ETD security solution can increase the security level of the entire organization through its ability to identify multiple vulnerabilities.

entry.title: How to get the HTML code of a website with SAP Build Process Automation?
entry.content: Hallo zusammen, ich versuche einen Prozess zu erstellen, um die Preise einiger Produkte zu prüfen. Dazu muss ich einige Links auf einer aufgezeichneten Website öffnen und den HTML-Code daraus abrufen. Ich finde in SAP BPA keine Aktivität, um den Code abzurufen. Hat jemand eine Idee, wie ich dieses Problem lösen kann?

entry.title: Re: Failed to load component for container - ModuleError:...
entry.content:
"Thanks for asking! I'm trying to load a component in my SAPUI5 application, but I'm running into an error. I've posted my manifest.json and PersonalDetails.js code above, but I forgot to include the XML code. Let me add that in.

pnl.view.xml

<mvc:View xmlns:form="sap.ui.layout.form" controllerName="employeeapplication.controller.pnl"
	xmlns:personal="employeeapplication.SharedBlocks.PersonalDetails"
	> 
<ObjectPageSection id="_IDGenObjectPageSection235" titleUppercase="false" title="Finance Project Details">
            <subSections>
                <ObjectPageSubSection id="financeDetailsId8" titleUppercase="false"  >
                    <blocks>
                     <personal:PersonalDetails id="_IDGenPersonalDetails1"/>
                    </blocks>
                </ObjectPageSubSection>
            </subSections>
        </ObjectPageSection>

 

sharedBlocks/personalDetails/PersonalDetails.view.xml

<mvc:View xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" xmlns:forms="sap.ui.layout.form" xmlns="sap.m">
<form:SimpleForm id="_IDGenSimpleForm131" 
						editable="false"
					layout="ResponsiveGridLayout"
					labelSpanL="1"
					labelSpanM="2"
					emptySpanL="0"
					emptySpanM="0"
					columnsL="0"
					columnsM="0">
					<form:content>
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabe132" text="Employee Id" />
						<m:Input id="_IDGenText1213" value="{Empid}"  enabled="false" />
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabel231" text="Currency" />
						<m:Input  id="_IDGenText11223" value="{Curr}" enabled="false" />
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabe1243" text="CTC" />
						<m:Input id="_IDGenText11233" value="{Ctc}" enabled="false" />
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabel2231" text="Gross Salary " />
						<m:Input id="_IDGenText112231" value="{GrossSal}" enabled="false" />
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabel2831" text="Days " />
						<m:Input id="_IDGenText112241" value="{Day_S}" enabled="false" />
						<m:Label id="_IDGenLabel2241" text="Joining Date" />
						<m:Input id="_IDGenText112251" value="{JoiningData}" enabled="false" />
					</form:content>
						</form:SimpleForm>

</mvc:View>

I'm trying to figure out what's causing the issue and how to fix it.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict the IP which can connect to SAP S/4 H...
entry.content: For an SAP HANA Cloud database, there is an allowlist that can be configured. For additional details see https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-security-guide/network-security

entry.title: ABAPConf South Africa 2024
entry.content:
Join us for the ultimate gathering of ABAP enthusiasts in South Africa! Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this conference is the perfect opportunity to expand your knowledge, network with like-minded professionals, and stay up-to-date with the latest trends in ABAP development. Get ready for a day filled with insightful sessions. Don't miss out on this fantastic event - mark your calendars now!

Find more information and register here!

*Please note that this event entry is a summarized version of the original event and your registration may be required by the organizer shared on the original event website. RSVP accuracy here is without guarantee and does not constitute registration. However, if you want to earn an SAP Community badge, please leave your RSVP here as well.

entry.title: Re: Activity Type ISO Code
entry.content:
Hi @burhanuzer,

I have an update related to your question. From 2408 Release changing units of measurement was opened for configuration. This is configuration activity "Check Units of Measurement".
Select dimension "Time" from the drop-down next to button "Units of Measurement". Click button "Units of Measurement". Then double-click unit of measurement STD (STD has commercial format HR) and remove the primary flag in section ALE/EDI. Then navigate to unit of measurement H and set the primary flag. Make sure that an ISO Code (recommended: HUR) is maintained for H.

It is recommended to decide for using H or HR and not to use both in parallel to avoid confusion.

Kind regards
Franziska

entry.title: Re: Finance issue
entry.content: Added Finance tag.

entry.title: Re: Custom field added by extending Manage Service Entry Sheet are not getting updated

yes that solved the issue, never saw or used this field before. Thanks!

This will really help our HR Department in generating job and comp info reports on a monthly basis for personnel cost planning.

 

Kind regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Integrate LinkedIn Learning courses with SuccessFactors Learning (LMS)- Part 2
entry.content:
No i havent had anyone come back to me on how to manage the LiL content most efficiently 

so the steps seem to be:

OCN content Import whats not already imported (weekly manual task)

Add to LinkedIn LMS library (in LMS)

Im not sure how you disable courses once they are unpublished in LiL - i.e. LiL no longer host them but you have the course imported and linked to a library. this will cause issues 

the synching and maintenance of the LMS to LiL content is clunky and wont deliver a good user experience so we are still discussing options here!

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Thanks for designing the exciting exercises. Here is my outcome for 2nd week

entry.title: CDS Annotation Semantics.dateTime causes time zone change
entry.content:
Hey Experts,

I have a view extension where I create a timestamp:

 

extend view I_LogisticsOrder with ZX_MyViewExtA
{
  // Full timestamp for display
  concat( afko.getri, afko.geuzi ) as ZConfOrdFinDateTime
}

 

Now I want to show this timestamp as a proper date the list. It works but the time shown is in UTC and not in the correct time zone. If I remove the @Semantics.dateTime: true it shows the correct time stamp. There are ways to convert the time to a time zone but I dont want to add unnecessary complexity.

 

extend view C_ObjPgMaintOrder with ZX_MAINTORDEXT
...
{
...
    @ui: {
      lineItem: [{ position: 1346, importance: #HIGH }]
    }
    @EndUserText.label: 'Confirmed Order Finish Date/Time'
    @Semantics.dateTime: true
    _MaintenanceOrder._Order.ZConfOrdFinDateTime as ZConfOrdFinDateTime
}

 

Any ideas how to deal with this?

entry.title: Re: Failed to load component for container - ModuleError:...
entry.content: Why it is not in the webapp folder?

entry.title: Re: SAP BW 7.5 to SAP Datasphere: Why It’s Time to Skip SAP BW/4HANA and Embedded Analytics
entry.content: OK. Correction. Embedded analytics in S/4, if implemented directly without separated reporting system, then it makes sense (time to implement, benefit to the users, ...). Implementing it as a in-between solution prior to DSP, is what I agree that it makes no sense. Same as moving first to BW/4 just to use the BW/4 Model Transfer option.

entry.title: Re: PR/PO Flexible Workflow with role Assignment
entry.content:
Hi,

You don't need to activate HR module however you have an option to activate HR mini-Master data, but I would recommend create a Z table where you can maintain User ID, Positions and DOA limit. You need to work with developer and that Position can be determined by BADI (Agent Determined by BADI) based on Dollar value. 

If you are determining the workflow based on Cost centers, then you need to add Cost centers in the Z table as well.

 

Thanks,

Sanan Afridi

entry.title: Re: Massachusetts - Supplemental vs Base Wages
entry.content:
Hi Euna -

We are still OP - not SaaS.

Our issue is that for MA, the supplemental wages are being added to base wages and causing the taxes to be overstated. We are being told to remove the cumulation 14 (base wages for BSI) from our supplemental wage type.  The issue we have with this is that impacts all tax authorities and not just MA and we have been under the impression that all taxable wage types should have this cumulation marked.

entry.title: Re: SAP provided official document type list
entry.content:
Hi,

You can get the list of document types from SPRO>MM>Purchasing>Document Types.

Thanks,

Sanan

entry.title: Monetary rewards approval workflow
entry.content:
Hi team - Curious to know if there’s a way to turn off approval workflow for monetary rewards at a certain level (e.g., nominators or approvers at a certain level). Or is there a way to reroute approval workflow to proxy when a proxy is specified?

Thanks!
Kevin

entry.title: Unable to Create Well (T code : O3U_WL1) - SAP S/4 HANA PRA(Production & Revenue Accounting) Module
entry.content:
I have enabled the Business Function for PRA system. And trying to create Well but need help in number range configuration. 

Error 1. And Important table is missing for creating well. 

Error 2:  I have done the configuration looking into the attached config document.

https://help.sap.com/doc/63892a011ca4430a8a97acdb072c4701/2023/en-US/PRA_ConfigurationGuide_v1_4.pdf

Can any one help to fix, I have done the configuration based on the above document link. But for few table there are no proper documents.

entry.title: Re: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
@HarshAgarwal To my understanding there is no additional cost when spinning up EMIS. The charging should be fully consumption based.

Details in the note: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2942344 

Best,

Karsten

entry.title: Adding smartfilterbars and button on Table Toolbar to standard Fiori app using Adaptation Project
entry.content:
In this blog we will learn SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects to extend a standard SAP Fiori application by generating and making simple changes to an application,  how to add custom fields in smartfilterbar section and button on Table Header toolbar. 

 Pre-requisites:

When we are creating adaptation project, you must have access of below: 

1. Business Application studio.

2. On-Premise system.

3. The app type must be Fiori elements. If you want to know the Fiori app type - go to the Fiori app reference library. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Create the Fiori Adaptation Project 

We will create adaptation project for Manage Sales Order - F1873. 

  • Go to Business Application Studio and Create project template as Adaptation Project and Start.

 

 

 

 

  • Select the Target environment: ABAP

 

 

 

 

                                                                          

  • Project the basic information like Project Name, Application Title, Namespace.                                           

  • Provide the configuration details of OnPremise system and click on Finish button.                           

   

 

 

 

 

  •  Project folder and all dependencies are installed in BAS tool.     

                                                                     

 

 

 

 

 

  • Add custom OData service in Adaptation Project -> Right click on the Project -> Adaptation Project.

  • Now we can see in the project directory that a folder named Changes has been added with file for OData Model.             

          

  • Run the Fiori app - To run the adaptation project --> Right click on Project --> Adaptation Project --> Open preview.   

                                                                                     

  • Now the standard fiori app can be edited and changed according to the client requirement. To make new changes, follow the steps as given in the screenshot below.

 

 

 

 

 

 

                                                                                   

  • Open the Fiori app in Edit mode.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                

 

 

 

 

 

  • Scenario: As per the requirements:  - Add Button on Table level – Add a Button - Select All (When the user clicks on the button, all the table rows should be selected).
  • To Create a button, we will select the table header and toolbar -> Right click à Create a fragment and corresponding controller.                                     

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Click on Add fragment --> create a fragment -  selectRow.fragment.xml file under fragments folder. Add a button in the fragment.                                                       

 

                            

 

 

 

  • create a onOpenDialogpress function in extension controller file - changes.js, to select all rows from table with validation. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • When no row is selected then messagebox popup will open.                                                            

                                                       

 

 

 

  • Add custom smartfilterbars for F4 valuehelp and Dropdown for Plant and overall status fields using fragment.                                                                                                                                                      

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Add XML code for Dropdown and F4valuehelp in smartfbar.fragment.xml.

 

 

 

 

 

  • To open valuehelp on Plant field smartfilterbar, Write the logic ( function: onPlantVH) in extension controller changes.js     

                                                                             

 

 

 

 

 

  • Create a PlantVH.fragment.xml to open the valuehelp on Plant Filterbar, as done in previous step.      

 

 

 

 

  • Smartfilterbars will get added for Plant and pausedOrder Field.        

    

 

 

  • Add Logic in extension controller changes.js to get the data in table based on custom and standard filters.

         If you want to know more about controller extension file or adaptation Fiori app. Please read from the                SAP SDK docs.

Override extension functions

  • onInitSmartFilterBar
  • provideExtensionAppStateData
  • restoreExtensionAppStateData
  • ensureFieldsForSelect
  • addFilters

 

 

 

/***
@controller Name:sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport,
*@viewId:cus.sd.salesorders.manage::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_SalesOrderWl_F1873
*/
/*!
 * OpenUI5
 * (c) Copyright 2009-2023 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company.
 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 - see LICENSE.txt.
 */

sap.ui.define([
	'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
	'sap/m/MessageBox',
	'sap/ui/model/Filter',
	'sap/ui/model/FilterOperator',
	'sap/m/Token'
	],
	function (
		ControllerExtension,
		MessageBox,
		Filter,
		FilterOperator,
		Token
		// ,OverrideExecution
	) {
		"use strict";
		return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.app.variant2.Changes", {
			// metadata: {
			// 	// extension can declare the public methods
			// 	// in general methods that start with "_" are private
			// 	methods: {
			// 		publicMethod: {
			// 			public: true /*default*/ ,
			// 			final: false /*default*/ ,
			// 			overrideExecution: OverrideExecution.Instead /*default*/
			// 		},
			// 		finalPublicMethod: {
			// 			final: true
			// 		},
			// 		onMyHook: {
			// 			public: true /*default*/ ,
			// 			final: false /*default*/ ,
			// 			overrideExecution: OverrideExecution.After
			// 		},
			// 		couldBePrivate: {
			// 			public: false
			// 		}
			// 	}
			// },

			// // adding a private method, only accessible from this controller extension
			// _privateMethod: function() {},
			// // adding a public method, might be called from or overridden by other controller extensions as well
			// publicMethod: function() {},
			// // adding final public method, might be called from, but not overridden by other controller extensions as well
			// finalPublicMethod: function() {},
			// // adding a hook method, might be called by or overridden from other controller extensions
			// // override these method does not replace the implementation, but executes after the original method
			// onMyHook: function() {},
			// // method public per default, but made private via metadata
			// couldBePrivate: function() {},
			// // this section allows to extend lifecycle hooks or override public methods of the base controller
			// override: {
			// 	/**
			// 	 * Called when a controller is instantiated and its View controls (if available) are already created.
			// 	 * Can be used to modify the View before it is displayed, to bind event handlers and do other one-time initialization.
			// 	 * @memberOf customer.app.variant2.Changes
			// 	 */
			// 	onInit: function() {
			// 	},

			// 	/**
			// 	 * Similar to onAfterRendering, but this hook is invoked before the controller's View is re-rendered
			// 	 * (NOT before the first rendering! onInit() is used for that one!).
			// 	 * @memberOf customer.app.variant2.Changes
			// 	 */
			// 	onBeforeRendering: function() {
			// 	},

			// 	/**
			// 	 * Called when the View has been rendered (so its HTML is part of the document). Post-rendering manipulations of the HTML could be done here.
			// 	 * This hook is the same one that SAPUI5 controls get after being rendered.
			// 	 * @memberOf customer.app.variant2.Changes
			// 	 */
			// 	onAfterRendering: function() {
			// 	},

			// 	/**
			// 	 * Called when the Controller is destroyed. Use this one to free resources and finalize activities.
			// 	 * @memberOf customer.app.variant2.Changes
			// 	 */
			// 	onExit: function() {
			// 	},

			// 	// override public method of the base controller
			// 	basePublicMethod: function() {
			// 	}
			// }

			_getODataModel: function () {
                return this.getView().getModel("customer.sales");
            },

			onOpenDialogPress: function () {
				// Create a confirmation dialog
				if (this.getView().byId("cus.sd.salesorders.manage::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_SalesOrderWl_F1873--responsiveTable").getItems().length === 0 || this.getView().byId("cus.sd.salesorders.manage::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_SalesOrderWl_F1873--responsiveTable").getItems().length === undefined) {
					sap.m.MessageBox.error("Please click on Go button to fetch the table data.");
					return;
				}
				var oDialog = new sap.m.Dialog({
				  title: "Confirmation",
				  type: "Message",
				  content: new sap.m.Text({
					text: "Please click on Go button to fetch the table data."
				  }),
				  beginButton: new sap.m.Button({
					text: "Ok",
					press: function () {
						
						var oTable = this.getView().byId("cus.sd.salesorders.manage::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_SalesOrderWl_F1873--responsiveTable");
						var aItems = oTable.getItems();
						aItems.forEach(function(oItem) {
							oItem.setSelected(true);		
				});
				oDialog.close();
					}.bind(this)
				  }),
				  endButton: new sap.m.Button({
					text: "Cancel",
					press: function () {
					  // Handle 'No' button press
					  oDialog.close();
					}
				  }),
				  afterClose: function () {
					oDialog.destroy();
				  }
				});
		  
				// Open the dialog
				oDialog.open();
			  },

			  onPlantVH: function () {
				this._getDialog().open();
			},
			_getDialog: function (oEvent) {
				if (!this.pDialog) {
					this.pDialog = sap.ui.xmlfragment(
						"customer.app.variant2.changes.fragments.PlantVH", this
					);
					this.getView().addDependent(this.pDialog);
				}
				return this.pDialog;
			},
			onPlantSearch:function (oEvent) {
				var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
				var oFilter = new Filter("VALUE", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
				var oBinding = oEvent.getSource().getBinding("items");
				oBinding.filter([oFilter]);
			},
			onPlantVHClose: function(oEvent) {
				var aSelectedItems = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItems"),
					oMultiInput = this.byId("idPlantComboBox");
				if (aSelectedItems && aSelectedItems.length > 0) {
					aSelectedItems.forEach(function (oItem) {
						oMultiInput.addToken(new Token({
							text: oItem.getCells()[0].getText()
						}));
					});
				}
			},
			override:{
				onBeforeRendering: function () {
					
					let oFilter = this.getView().byId("cus.sd.salesorders.manage::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_SalesOrderWl_F1873--listReportFilter");
					oFilter.attachInitialise(function (oEvent) {
						let aFiltersItems = oEvent.getSource().getAllFilterItems();
						aFiltersItems.forEach((item) => {
							// not showing the custom added filter in filterbar for Plant
							if (item.getName() === "Plant" || item.getName() === "Paused_order") {
								item.setVisibleInFilterBar(false);
							}	
						});
					}.bind(this));
				},

				
				
				templateBaseExtension:{
					addFilters: function (fnAddFilter) {

						// custom filter for Plant
						var oPlant = this.byId("idPlantComboBox"),
							aSelectedTokens = oPlant.getTokens();

						if (aSelectedTokens.length > 0) {
							var aFilter, aFilters = [];

							aSelectedTokens.forEach((token) => {
								aFilter = new sap.ui.model.Filter({
									path: "Plant",
									operator: sap.ui.model.FilterOperator.EQ,
									value1: token
								});

								if (oPlant.getTokens().length > 1) {
									aFilters.push(aFilter);
								}
							});

							let aFinalFilter;
							if (aFilters.length > 0) {
								aFinalFilter = new sap.ui.model.Filter({
									filters: aFilters
								});
							} else {
								aFinalFilter = aFilter;
							}

							if (aFinalFilter) {
								fnAddFilter(this, aFinalFilter);
							}
						}

						// Custom Filter for Paused Order
						var oPausedOrder = this.byId("idPaused_orderComboBox"),
							aSelectedTokens = oPausedOrder.getSelectedKeys();

						if (aSelectedTokens.length > 0) {
							var aFilter, aFilters = [];

							aSelectedTokens.forEach((token) => {
								aFilter = new sap.ui.model.Filter({
									path: "Paused_order",
									operator: sap.ui.model.FilterOperator.EQ,
									value1: token
								});

								if (oPausedOrder.getSelectedKeys().length > 1) {
									aFilters.push(aFilter);
								}
							});

							let aFinalFilter;
							if (aFilters.length > 0) {
								aFinalFilter = new sap.ui.model.Filter({
									filters: aFilters
								});
							} else {
								aFinalFilter = aFilter;
							}

							if (aFinalFilter) {
								fnAddFilter(this, aFinalFilter);
							}
						}
					},

					
					provideExtensionAppStateData: function (fnSetAppStateData) {
						var oPlant = this.byId("idPlantComboBox"),
						aSelectedTokens = oPlant.getTokens();
						fnSetAppStateData(this, {
							customApprovalFilter: aSelectedTokens
						});

						var oPausedOrder = this.byId("idPaused_orderComboBox"),
						aSelectedPausedOrder = oPausedOrder.getSelectedKeys();
						fnSetAppStateData(this, {
							customApprovalFilter: aSelectedPausedOrder
						});
					},
					restoreExtensionAppStateData: function (fnGetAppStateData) {
						var oExtensionData = fnGetAppStateData(this);
						if (oExtensionData) {
							this.byId("idPlantComboBox").setTokens(oExtensionData.customApprovalFilter);
							this.byId("idPaused_orderComboBox").setSelectedKeys(oExtensionData.customApprovalFilter);
						}
				}
				
			}
		}
		});
	});

 

 

 

 

  • Deployment of Adaptation Project

        Right click on Project/webapp folder --> select Adaptation Project --> Open Deployment Wizard.                

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Select the system, where you want to deploy.

 

 

 

  • Provide the package information: 

 

 

 

 

  • Provide the Workbench TR and deploy the adaptation project. 

Note: If new changes come from your client side, after making changes in the application, delete the existing project from the gateway system to redeploy and follow the below step.

Go to SE38 --> run the report (/UI5/DEL_ADAPTATION_PROJECT) --> execute --> Provide the app variant ID --> execute --> to delete the records provide the Transport No. --> select all entries from the table --> Press delete button. After completing the process we can redeploy the Fiori app same as given in deployment step.

 

 

Congratulation now you are running Adaptation project, try custom and standard Filterbar, and fetch the data based on the filters.

 

Keep learning & Keep Sharing!! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules (5/5)

While copying , the assigned responsibility rules are not copying by default.

 

Thanks,

Gowtham.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP ByDesign webservice : Query Employee Actual times
entry.content:
Hello, 

Is it really the case that this webservice is not supported at all when the status is "not released". ? I should be able to use the webservice to retrieve employee timesheets for specific time periods and then delete those timesheets. There are several thousand rows involved, so manual deletion is not an option. As far as I understand, there is no possibility for mass deletions in the UI either, so webservice is the only option here.

I tried this webservice via SOAP, but the errors just seemed to indicate that this webservice is not supported. Other possible supported web services can also be pointed out. 

Any tips on how to deal with this? 

 

Thanks in advance,

"scheduleID": 3750,
"userID": "User-1",
"segmentID": 1,
"comments": "User Attended Segment"
},
{
"scheduleID": 3750,
"userID": "User-2",
"segmentID": 1,

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content:
Sounds like you simply need to create an SQL Expression. 
No need for a UFL for such a simple case.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: Field Extension Wizard in S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Nice blog post.

This feature is also available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment, though there  are no extensible RAP BO's available so far.  

However you can run in the trial systems a hands-on script that explains in depth the topic of developer extensibility.

abap-platform-rap630/README.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap630 (github.com)

Kind regards,

Andre

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2408?
entry.content:
As you may know by now, SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is a software-as-a-service (Saas) solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). This public cloud solution is perfectly suited for organizations seeking at kick-starting a master data management initiative at low entry barriers (see also the overview presentation). 

Full Functional Scope

For details of the complete functional scope, see the latest deep-dive presentation about SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition.

What's New?

Continuing the path of innovations outlined in my blog post on the 2405 release, the 2408 version comes with the following enhancements:

Enhancements to the core attributes of the business partner data model

You can now assign one or multiple organizational units such as purchasing organization, sales area, or company code to a business partner. This feature comprises the assignment of organizational units only. It does not include further organizational attributes like reconciliation accounts, incoterms, or payment methods, etc. Features include:

  • Option to create an organizational unit, e.g. purchasing organization, sales area, or company code, directly when creating a business partner using the quick create view or the object page​
  • Assignment of multiple organizational units
  • At least one supplier or customer role is required to create a supplier or customer organizational unit.​
  • All MDG processes support the organizational unit assignment.​
  • Possibility to authorize users to create and/or display organizational units
  • Possibility to upload the business configuration from the backend system to SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition via spreadsheet
  • Possibility to define validation rules for organizational units

Fig.: Assignment of organizational units to supplier record

You can exchange organizational units (purchasing organizations and company codes) for suppliers between SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition and other applications using SOAP.

Inbound process integration of business-partner relationships

You can exchange business partner relationships between SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition and other applications using SOAP. For this purpose, the new SAP_BX_DCM and SAP_BX_DMU process templates are available. Please note that you can only exchange business partner relationships with systems that support time-dependent business partner relationships. 

Key Benefits

These innovations increase the scope of action for data stewards:

  • through added business content in data models.
  • added support for decentralized maintenance now including relationships.

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Well (T code : O3U_WL1) - SAP S/4 HANA PRA(Production & Revenue Accounting)
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: ECC with SFIN on Hana Archiving
entry.content:
The best option is to keep in on-prem, and powered off.

Turn the system on by request when someone needs to access the data.

entry.title: Create A Mobile Attendance Application
entry.content:
Hi folks

I'm new to SAP Builder or AppGyver and will like to build a simple mobile attendance app for a small group.

The attendance app just need only local storage.  

Will need only items such as the following:

Name               Department        Status

 Michael         Sales                   Working

Jean              Marketing            MC

Mary             Production          Annuel Leave

Will like to know how to link the logic behind the buttons.

Meanwhile, any advice from you will be greatly appreciated.

Thank you with regards.

Best regards

Johnny

entry.title: Re: Insertion of extra line item in delivery creation
entry.content:
Topic is really old but people are still searching:
Solution:
CALL FUNCTION 'RV_DELIVERY_CREATE'
      EXPORTING 
....
      is_delivery_extend = ls_deliv_extend       "here put the delivery which you want to add another item

entry.title: RAP630 - Cloud ERP Extensibility - Updated using two new ADT based wizards
entry.content:
Since last SAP TechEd there was a script available that shows how to leverage developer extensibility using ABAP cloud called  RAP630 - Cloud ERP Extensibility .

abap-platform-rap630/README.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap630 (github.com)

Since meanwhile two new ADT based wizards have been released for ABAP cloud you can walk now much faster through this script and it also shows a new feature of letting you generate your custom UI on top of a C1-released RAP business object.

The first wizard lets you simply right-click on a C1-release RAP business object:

 

Create new bank

Whereas the second wizard, the new field extension wizard lets you add fields to an extensible RAP business object.

In order to let several developers test this new feature the RAP630 script contains a class that generates an extensible RAP BO for your convenience in the customer Z-namespace based on my openSource RAP Generator. 

By simply right clicking on the projection view of the released RAP business object the generator will generate all required repository objects.

 

If you want to try out this yourself either go and get a user in a SAP BTP ABAP Environment trial system. 

Create an SAP BTP ABAP Environment Trial User | SAP Tutorials

An example how to use the field extension wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud has been shown by the community member taranam in the following blog post:

S/4HANA Cloud: Field Extension Wizard in S/4HANA C... - SAP Community

Kind regards,   

Andre

entry.title: Utilize Requirement Modeling in Software Engineering for Functional Specification [FS]
entry.content:
Introduction

The functional specification is very crucial for requirement documentation and management when all the functional and technical SAP consultants work on SAP RICEFW during the realization of any SAP project.

In this blog post I would like to discuss about requirements engineering standard practices and requirement modeling in high level which can be utilized for Functional Specification derived from requirements documentation.

Requirements Engineering:

  • is a set of procedures used to establish the customer requirements from a system including the constraints under which it operates and is developed.
  • is a well organized approach to eliciting, organizing, and documenting the requirements of the system, and a set of procedures that formulates and maintains agreement between the customer and the project team on the changing requirements of the system.

Requirements are:

  • Statements of needs from a expected system.
  • System features and constraints characterization that are identified while the requirements elicitation.

Requirements Activities:

Following diagram depicted activities of requirement engineering process in a high level.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Requirements Types:

Requirements can be classified under following ways.

  • Functional requirements
    • Basic subject matter how behave the system and are measured by concrete means such as data values, logic for decision making and algorithms.
  • Non-functional requirements
    • Behavioral properties that the required functions must have, such as performance and scalability, reliability, etc.
  • Project constraints
    • Recognize the ways of final product must suitable into the world.
    • Integration with some existing hardware, software or business practice.
    • Defined project scope.
  • Project drivers
    • Business- related forces.
    • Purpose of the product is a project driver, as are all of the stakeholders.
  • Project issues
    • Define the conditions under which the project will be done.
    • Included to present a coherent picture of all factors contributing to the success or failure.

IEEE 830 Documentation Standard for a Software Requirements Specification (1998):

Criteria for a high-quality requirements set are:

  • Unambiguous: Every statement has unique interpretation. Terms should be clear and well defined.
  • Complete: All major requirements are considered. No items have been disregarded for future definition.
  • Verifiable: All functionalities defined as part of the project have a corresponding test to determine whether they have been successfully delivered.
  • Consistent: Final product's end users benefit by getting a product that is easy to use enhancing the user experience.
  • Modifiable: The cost of change and refers to the ease with which a software system can accommodate changes.
  • Traceable: Uniquely identification is required for each referenced requirement.
  • Correct: Every stated requirement represents something required of the system to be built.

Requirements Modelling:

Unified Modeling Language (UML)  and Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) can be used for requirement modeling with graphical representation by diagrams. You can freely use draw.io online application to prepare diagrams.

  • Functional Requirements
    • Hierarchy
    • Data Flow Diagrams
    • Use Cases
    • Activity Diagrams
    • Other graphical models
  • Data Requirements
    • Entity relationship diagram
  • Linking data and functional requirements
  • Maintaining the link between textual and graphical requirements documents

Benefits of Models:

  • Provide a visual, non-technical representation
  • Are easy to understand
  • Quick to produce and easy to amend
  • Use limited number of symbols with specific meanings
    • implicit presentation of information
    • concise system descriptions
    • enable code generation through CASE
  • Minimize redundancy of information
    • effects of changes are easily identified and incorporated
  • Use a simple, top-down expansion
    • easy progression between levels of detail
    • convenient review of the overall system structure

Good Requirements Guidelines:

  • Add simple direct sentences
    • As short as possible but no shorter
  • Add a limited vocabulary
    • Project glossary also recommend
  • Focus on results (goal)
  • Define verifiable criteria
    • later relate to Acceptance Criterion

In conclusion, with above blog post your are able to get an overview idea of requirement engineering paradigm and possible opportunities to utilize them with sap RICEFW requirement modeling.

Hope you have gained something it and stay tuned for real world scenario with simple examples in my future blog.

Could you please help.

entry.title: Re: Price items in Service Transactions
entry.content:
Hi Jane, this feature is only available with Item-based accounting (the new Finance integration for Service Contracts and Service Orders). Item-based Accounting only supports Event-based Revenue recognition (EBRR). 

Regards, Gert

entry.title: Re: MDF Multiple Changes Per Day_Boomi taking only latest record for integration
entry.content: Hey @jaisonpj now I know it has been quite some time, but have you found the solution to this issue?

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Depreciation areas for assets
entry.content:
Thanks for putting in the solution you found yourself 🙂

For customer defined Asset Classes you need to add the Valuation Areas to the new Asset Class. The below link contains all the AA customizing points for reference. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/227f0f8f970a44f88d6c1b875971c306.html?locale=en-US

Make Valuation View-Dependent Settings for Asset Class (102609)

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Depreciation areas for assets
entry.content: Also for reference the error is AA025 No depreciation areas have been defined for asset &-&

entry.title: How to change the value of a parameter used in several selection screens
entry.content:
The below program has 2 selection screens 1000 and 1001, both having the same parameter DUMMY, and we go from 1000 to 1001 by pressing F8 (Execute), and back to 1000 by pressing F3 (Back).

If I type a value in the parameter DUMMY in the selection screen 1001, press Enter, go back to the selection screen 1000, this value is not shown in the selection screen 1000, and by debug we can see that the global variable DUMMY has been cleared by the system.

How to retain the parameter value?

For information, if I type the value in the selection screen 1000 and go to the selection screen 1001, the value is shown. It seems to be a backward-only "issue".

REPORT zzsro_selection_screen_include.

TABLES sscrfields.

PARAMETERS dummy.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 1001.
  SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT /1(83) text1001.
  SELECTION-SCREEN INCLUDE PARAMETERS dummy.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 1001.

INITIALIZATION.
  text1001 = 'Screen 1001'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1000' AND sscrfields-ucomm = 'ONLI'.
    sscrfields-ucomm = ''.
    CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001.
  ENDIF.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1001' AND sscrfields-ucomm = 'CRET'.
    sscrfields-ucomm = ''. " Execute -> stays on 1001
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Getting 500 Error when Trying to execute POST request in Automation Pilot
entry.content:
I'm trying to use the HttpRequest to run an Incentive Management Pipeline using POST request.  The command uses the clientId, clientSecret, body with the parameters needed, and the tokenUrl.

I can run the request in Postman successfully but in Automation Pilot, I'm getting a 500 (Internal Server Error) response.

When I run a GET request, such as searching for a specific period, it completes successfully.  Not with the POST request.

How can I figure out what's causing this error so that I can fix it?  Any and all suggestions are welcome.  😊

These APIs are in GCP.

Thanks in advance!

Ron

entry.title: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am looking to create/configure a bot in BPA for SAP Business ByDesign.

This bot has to locate the projects (KUT extension is added to projects of type INDICATOR) that are ready to be assigned with a team on the Header. The team information is available on an EXCEL sheet (placed in an FTP/Sharepoint site).

Can I develop/configure such a bot in BPA to connect to ByDesign, Retrieve the Projects, and then connect to Sharepoint to access the file assign the team, and offset the indicator on the projects automatically in one shot?

I also want to know how to trigger the bot, I mean which of the 3 types would be the appropriate one to trigger the process in the BPA so the bot can do its job?

#SAPBusinessByDesign #ByDesign #BPA #SAPBPA #SAPByD #BTP

entry.title: Re: How to change the value of a parameter used in severa...
entry.content:
When leaving a selection screen, the parameters are saved by the kernel to the memory, and when coming back to the selection screen, they are restored from the memory.

Add the below code, that will replace the values in memory with the current parameter values, when leaving the selection screen 1001 to get back to the selection screen 1000. That's an "evil" workaround and may stop working at any time if SAP does a change of the "kernel" or any system ABAP program (RSDBSPVA, RSDBRUNT, etc.) so better redesign your program to use any other supported solution.

 

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON EXIT-COMMAND.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1001'.
    PERFORM export_variables.
  ENDIF.

FORM export_variables.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS <restore_memkey> TYPE RSVAMEMKEY.
  ASSIGN ('(RSDBSPVA)RESTORE-MEMKEY') TO <restore_memkey>.
  PERFORM %_export_var_to_mem IN PROGRAM (sy-repid) USING <restore_memkey>.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Payroll Posting to Account with Business Partner
entry.content:
Dear Experts:

We are facing the issue during payroll posting to account that multiple employees are getting posted to single Business Partner whereas each employee has their respective Business partner created in system with same employee number.

Screen short is given below to elaborate the issue, showing that employees’ numbers 10000145 and 10000146 are mapped against their own business partner, whereas the loan amount of both employees are posted to 10000145 only.

entry.title: Re: Planned order created from sales order even if there...
entry.content:
Hello

Very often this is an issue with your rescheduling check. Either production orders are not included in the rescheduling check, of this specific order is outside the rescheduling horizon. 

Check this WIKI for more details about the rescheduling check:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697863.html

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Payroll Posting to Account with Business Partner
entry.content: Dear Tariq Please ensure symbolic account are configure properly,

entry.title: Re: I am unable post deferred tax transfer documents in T...
entry.content:
Hi sir, hope you are well. 

The solution for this problem is in the Tax codes, you need to review the configuration of the tax rates that are in the tax codes. 

For example I use the tax code 2C / 16% but also when I execute the deferred tax transfer it points to the 3C / 16% tax code.

You need to verify that the tax rate is maintained in both tax codes.

Best Regards. 

-Michel Garcia.

entry.title: Re: Digital Discovery Assessment - The guided decision between SAP S/4HANA Public or Private Cloud E
entry.content:
Hello dears,

Can you please help me answering this question? Thank you very much!

  1. Which information does the SAP Digital Discovery Assessment provide? Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.
  • A. Localizations and legal entities
  • B. Draft project schedule and roadmap
  • C. List of recommended scope items
  • D. Known integration requirements

entry.title: Re: Consume HANA views in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Thank you. Even i was thinking why 2 warehouses. Your comments clarify. Thank you.

entry.title: More Enhancements for SAP Community
entry.content:
Although it's been more than a month since our last announcement about enhancements (meaning improvements and bug fixes), I'm happy to report that we recently rolled out some fairly significant updates to the platform -- including perhaps the thing most demanded by members. Specifically…

Additional Sorting Options for SAP Managed Tags

Since the migration to the new platform, members have been asking for a way to sort SAP Managed Tag feeds by content type (e.g., blog posts vs. Q&A). Now members can do that and more -- sorting by all types of posts available within SAP Community, as well as by creation date, activity (e.g., most commented), and area within the platform (e.g., a specific Q&A board).

See for yourself. Visit any SAP Managed Tag page and try out the various options until you're able to find the content that most appeals to you.

More Prominent Placement of SAP Managed Tags

Another adjustment related to SAP Managed Tags: They now appear at the top of a post (e.g., question, blog), so readers can identify immediately what the content is about.

In the previous layout, the tags appeared at the end of the post, and members commented that they'd like this information sooner. Now they get it right away -- at the beginning of the post.

Better Log-In Experience

If you are one of the members who complained about the constant need to log in each and every time you visited us here, then you may have already noticed this change.

With this improvement, when you log in, you stay logged in for 30 days -- as long as you don't log out/clear your cache.

This improvement goes across platforms, so if you log in to SAP.com, support.sap.com, or learning.sap.com, you'll log in automatically when navigating to SAP Community. The same applies if you log in to SAP Community before going to SAP.com.

Group Curators Easier to Identify

If you visit a lot of areas in SAP Community, you may have thought that my colleagues and I ran just about every group -- because our names often appeared next to "Owned by" on every landing page.

In actuality, due to the way that the platform was set up, the person who created the group would appear as its primary owner, and since group creation is one of my team's responsibilities, my colleagues and I would show up as group owners -- but it's really the curators who oversee the groups once we set them up.

This approach made things a bit confusing for visitors who wanted to connect with the members who were actually managing a group. Not surprisingly, they'd end up contacting my colleagues and me.

Now each group shows its curators as the owners, so visitors can easily identify the members who kindly volunteer to run groups for specific locations, interests, and so on.  (I mean, sure, I'd love to talk to you about the awesome Melbourne group, but you're better off reaching out to my friend @pjcools, who is doing the work there. So it's better you see his name as the group owner now instead of mine!)

Clearer Options for Blog Boards

As explained in this post, our FAQ, the how-to videos, and our official SAP Community tutorial, bloggers who want to share content in the Products and Technology areas need to select between either "by Members" or "by SAP" boards (depending on whether they work for SAP). Still, it was not uncommon for bloggers to select the wrong board -- only to be greeted by this error message as a result:

Now, to avoid that experience, the Create a Blog button will be grayed out when bloggers attempt to post content in a board that isn't open to their submissions. For example, as an SAP employee, I'll see this if I try to blog in the CRM and CX Blogs by Members board (because I should be posting in the CRM and CX Blogs by SAP board instead):

With this change, we hope our bloggers will be able to locate the correct boards more easily when they wish to share their knowledge with other community members.

Bullet Bug Be Gone!

I'll wrap up with a fix for something that had been bugging members.

Members had reported that when they added a nested bullet list within a bullet list, the system would automatically add an extra space. That's been fixed:

While that fix may not seem like a major thing, I know this bug had been aggravating people during the editing process, so I'm glad to pass along this news.

I'll be back later this week with an SAP Community Voice newsletter highlighting new and popular content. And as more improvements and fixes get introduced, I'll be reporting them here in What's New as well. So remember to subscribe to this board and keep an eye out for the latest announcements.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: Hi, my name is Hamza. I am Mechanical Engineering graduate with 3 years experience. I want to switch my career as we know that world and changing and one needs to keep up with world in order to survive as Darwin said Fit of the fittest. So I decided that it is the ideal age for me to learn modern tool such as SAP that will help me well in my future.

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules (5/5)
entry.content: @vksgowtham, you can send me an MR for Friday via email so we can discuss your requirements more and provide you with a better solution.

entry.title: Re: Actual dates & durations in AFC
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content: Thank you for the feedback. Of course I simplified my question as much as possible to focus on the root cause of my issue: Why can't I have an INT64 parameter in UFL function? Nonetheless, your suggestion of a SQL Expression for my TOSTRING(table.column) is a good one. I can reference that in my formula and it works great a runtime. The Designer though (I'm using CR 2016 at the moment) insists on doing a select TOSTRING(table.column) every time I look at or modify that SQL Expression. There are millions of rows in my table and this takes forever. Is there any way to suppress the design from doing that? I can't put a where clause on the SQL Expression because the real clause for the SQL is created at runtime.

entry.title: Re: Actual dates & durations in AFC
entry.content:
Any suggestion on this topic ?

This is for me a basic requirement which was possible in FCC but I cannot find an equivalent in AFC, nor any plan in the product roadmap.

entry.title: Re: GENERATIVE AI HUB SDK - Python Error
entry.content:
hi @lnog 

so happy to see progress!

Could be the case that your Langchain library got an update, and I might have provided also old instructions, but first make sure you update LangChain Version.

 
pip install --upgrade langchain
 
Instead of using the deprecated LLMChain class, the warning suggests using RunnableSequence. 1. Then lets replace the LLMChain usage with the suggested alternative.
 
# Old deprecated usage
chain = LLMChain(prompt=prompt, llm=llm)
# New recommended usage
chain = prompt | llm
 
2. Instead of using the deprecated Chain.run method, the warning recommends using invoke.
# Old deprecated usage
output = chain.run(input_data)
# New recommended usage
output = chain.invoke(input_data)
 
Let me know how it goes

entry.title: Re: Documentation help - CATA and how it posts time/labou...
entry.content: Refer to documentation "Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS)","Data Transfer to Project System (PS)" and Confirmation via Time Sheets (CATS).

entry.title: Cross-company Strock transfer order with Advanced return management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Most of the large companies run SAP and some data shows that 75% of world’s transaction happen in SAP. Most of these companies operate in several countries and there are transfer of their products from one country to another. This transfer of materials can occur through

  • classic intercompany or advanced intercompany stock transfer
  • advanced intercompany sales and advanced intercompany stock transfer

as shown in below image.

However, there may be situations that receiving company wants to return the received materials to the delivering company for different reasons like poor quality, over-delivery etc. SAP S/4HANA provides the option of cross-company stock transfer order to return the material to the delivering company using Advanced return management (ARM) as shown in below image.

Process starts with a

  1. Return cross-company stock transfer order with a flag on ‘Return item’

2. Outbound delivery is created from the return STO.

3. Inbound delivery is created from SPED output of the outbound delivery

4. Goods receipt happens with movement type 673 and stock moves to Quality inspection

5. Inspection certifies the material as OK

6. Stock is moved to Free available stock as Logistic follow up activity

7. Credit memo is created with respect to the inbound delivery

ARM also provides a Return overview with all the relevant documents and their statuses.

ARM provides an integrated and transparent return with automatic logistical follow up activities for all possible return scenarios.ARM also works well with EWM managed warehouses.

This blog is based on my personal insights, observation, and tests in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release in my preparation for Workshop on Reverse Logistics for Advanced intercompany flows for one of my projects. Will appreciate your views / comment and feedback.

However when I click on the Next > button, it comes with a blank Connection Settings window, at this time I should see the form when I can enter my creds.

Any thought friend what could be causing that behavior?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.14
entry.content:
@meganhoy The help file for the "apply advanced filters" section has a discrepancy between the filter definition section (2 quarters of actuals and 2 quarters of forecast) and the rest of the examples (1 quarter of actuals, 3 quarters of forecast)

Would it be possible to align either the table screenshots or the definition screenshot? It is a little confusing as it is right now. (If I am even understanding it correctly)

Apply Advanced Filters and Hide Unbooked Members to Create Table Layouts for Ad-Hoc Planning (Optimized) | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Explore the Newly Redesigned Cloud Card Tab!
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the newly redesigned Cloud tab in SAP for Me’s Finance & Legal Dashboard, now providing a single, comprehensive view of a customer’s | partner’s Cloud Contracts. This update combines the previous individual cards (Cloud Materials, Cloud Orders, and Cloud Contracts) into one streamlined view, making it easy for users to find all the details relating to the management of their Cloud contracts.

Key Features of the New Cloud Contracts Card:

Unified View: The Contracts card under the Cloud tab now consolidates all Customer | Partner cloud contracts, providing visibility to expiring contracts at the top, sorted by end date.

Retained Functionalities: Enjoy the same popular features such as search functionality, contract expiry notifications, and direct access to contract details.

Enhanced Capabilities: Users can customize their view by filtering, sorting, grouping, and adjusting columns as per their needs. 

New Cloud Card in SAP for Me

Important Note: The "Manage Contract" options depend on the contract status, so not all options may be available to every customer at all times. For any questions, please contact your SAP Representative.

For further details on navigating the new Cloud Contracts card, including how to access and utilize its features, please refer to the following resources:

  • My Renewals @ SAP for Me Customer Reference Guide: Comprehensive information about the new Cloud tab as well as access, authorizations, purchase order submission, cloud contract functionality, and more.
  • SAP for Me Online Help

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow for Maintenance in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Good afternoon:

In the Manage Work flow for Asset Management / maintenance order app, I have already set up a workflow rule so that if the planned cost is greater than $1000, it goes through an approval.

 

The maintenance order does enter authorization with the ORAI Order Approval In Process status. I look for the workflow in the inbox and it doesn't arrive.

 

My question is where can I approve the maintenance order?

Thank you for your response.

entry.title: Where to find SAP wide Forum for AI SAP specific topics?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

currently, we are working on an AI tool and I noticed that there are a ton of design decisions to be made and therefore a lot of research is necessary. 

My high level question is: Is there a platform for AI Developers coming from all the different LOBs to exchange information, asking questions about SAP specifics and learn from each other?

Is this blog/discussion the right place? Even if it is open for non-SAP readers?

Happy to hear your thoughts!

Max

entry.title: Re: Automatic approval for maintenance orders workflow is not working
entry.content: Good afternoon: I also have a similar issue in the Manage Workflow for Asset Management / maintenance order app. I have already set up a workflow rule so that if the planned cost is greater than $1000 it goes through an approval. The maintenance order does enter authorization with the ORAI Order Approval In Process status. I look for the workflow in the inbox and it doesn't arrive. My question is where can I approve the maintenance order? Thank you for your response.
